author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
The Seventh Calumny,—That Augustin Asserts that in Baptism All Sins are Not Remitted.
title: The Seventh Calumny,—That Augustin Asserts that in Baptism All Sins are Not Remitted.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Chapter 26 [XIII.]—The Seventh Calumny,—That Augustin Asserts that in Baptism All Sins are Not Remitted.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: “They also say,” says he, “that baptism does not give complete remission of sins, nor take away crimes, but that it shaves them off, so that the roots of all sins are retained in the evil flesh.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Who but an unbeliever can affirm this against the Pelagians?

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: I say, therefore, that baptism gives remission of all sins, and takes away guilt, and does not shave them off; and “that the roots of all sins are” not “retained in the evil flesh, as if of shaved hair on the head, whence the sins may grow to be cut down again.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: For it was I that found out that similitude, too, for them to use for the purposes of their calumny, as if I thought and said this.
